title: Author Correction: Global expansion of the ecological impact of extra-urban road traffic
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01888-7Author Correction: Global expansion of the ecological impact of extra-urban road traffic

title: Connecting the general and particular in interdisciplinary urban research
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00461-9Understanding urbanization trends, dynamics and environmental consequences is at the heart of charting sustainable futures in the 21st century. Interdisciplinary research is crucial to this, yet there often remains separation between the scales of urban analysis — searches for quantifiable, generalizable trends and detailed local, historical analyses — and between both scientific scales and policy. We argue for the importance of long-term institutional spaces and workflows that stimulate constant interaction between scalar perspectives and policy within a single epistemic, theory-based process to help to navigate an increasingly central way of human life.

title: Connecting the general and particular in interdisciplinary urban research
abstract: Understanding urbanization trends, dynamics and environmental consequences is at the heart of charting sustainable futures in the 21st century. Interdisciplinary research is crucial to this, yet there often remains separation between the scales of urban analysis.

title: Author Correction: Rising temperatures increase added sugar intake disproportionately in disadvantaged groups in the USA
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02689-8Author Correction: Rising temperatures increase added sugar intake disproportionately in disadvantaged groups in the USA

title: Human-driven sea-level rise has quadrupled the frequency of coastal sea-level extremes since 1900
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02659-0Sea-level rise in conjunction with storm surge and tidal variations leads to extreme sea levels that threaten coastal systems. Here the authors use tide-gauge data and models to quantify how anthropogenic climate change has increased the risk of these extreme sea-level events since 1900.

title: Increasing tropical cyclone rainfall and landslide risk in Southern California
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02633-wSouthern California is rarely affected by tropical cyclones at present. Here the authors show that this could change with warming, leading to an increase in landslide risk that is expected to disproportionately affect low-income households.

title: Roads less traveled converge at Bloomberg CityLab 2026
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00457-5Cities must innovate and, increasingly, lead in an increasingly breathless world. Nature Cities attended CityLab 2026 to learn from and with leaders and pioneers embracing the challenges together.

title: Digging into the costs of urban soil rehabilitation
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00452-wUrban soil rehabilitation is vital for sustainable cities, but its economic dimensions remain unexplored. This study estimates that the median costs in France range from €50 m−2 to over €800 m−2, highlighting the need for healthy soil preservation.

title: Human-driven sea-level rise has quadrupled the frequency of coastal sea-level extremes since 1900
abstract: Sea-level rise in conjunction with storm surge and tidal variations leads to extreme sea levels that threaten coastal systems. Here the authors use tide-gauge data and models to quantify how anthropogenic climate change has increased the risk of these extreme sea-level events since 1900.

title: Increasing tropical cyclone rainfall and landslide risk in Southern California
abstract: Southern California is rarely affected by tropical cyclones at present. Here the authors show that this could change with warming, leading to an increase in landslide risk that is expected to disproportionately affect low-income households.

title: Digging into the costs of urban soil rehabilitation
abstract: Urban soil rehabilitation is vital for sustainable cities, but its economic dimensions remain unexplored. This study estimates that the median costs in France range from 50 EUR/m2 to over 800 EUR/m2, highlighting the need for healthy soil preservation.

title: Current state of affairs
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 09 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02683-0As climate change impacts are increasingly apparent, there are changes in society and the political landscape that need to be considered.

title: Sustainable battery recycling through spatial and technological alignment
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01851-6Battery recycling has a crucial role in enabling circular economy and sustainable energy transition. This study presents a multi-scale analytical framework to assist the design of low-carbon, high-efficiency battery recycling systems.

title: Early signs that the EU carbon border adjustment mechanism is reshaping EU–India steel trade
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02607-yThe EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) was introduced to curb carbon leakage, but its impact is debated. Using export and emissions data from Indian steel plants, researchers show early evidence that the CBAM could penalize high-emission producers, rather than act as a blanket trade barrier.

title: A global early warning system for predicting exposure of biodiversity to extreme heat
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02642-9The authors develop an early warning system to predict the risks of extreme temperatures for 30,585 vertebrates 1–9 months in advance. They identify species and regions at risk and highlight the potential for early warning systems to maximize management activities that mitigate negative outcomes.

title: A clearer picture of who lives in the world’s cities
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00469-1A new city-level dataset examines how urban population structures have changed worldwide between 2000 and 2020. The work reveals divergent age distributions, sex ratios and migration patterns across more than 10,000 cities, and it provides evidence for locally tailored urban planning.

title: Madrid and the art of cities
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00458-4Madrid and the art of cities

title: Sustainable battery recycling through spatial and technological alignment
abstract: Battery recycling has a crucial role in enabling circular economy and sustainable energy transition. This study presents a multi-scale analytical framework to assist the design of low-carbon, high-efficiency battery recycling systems.

title: Early signs that the EU carbon border adjustment mechanism is reshaping EU-India steel trade
abstract: The EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) was introduced to curb carbon leakage, but its impact is debated. Using export and emissions data from Indian steel plants, researchers show early evidence that the CBAM could penalize high-emission producers, rather than act as a blanket trade barrier.

They identify species and regions at risk and highlight the potential for early warning systems to maximize management activities that mitigate negative outcomes.

title: A clearer picture of who lives in the world cities
abstract: A new city-level dataset examines how urban population structures have changed worldwide between 2000 and 2020. The work reveals divergent age distributions, sex ratios and migration patterns across more than 10,000 cities.

title: Uniting forces for green carbon chemicals
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01852-5The shift towards renewable carbon for producing everyday chemicals hinges on innovative strategies to utilize sustainable feedstocks. By synergizing catalysis and reaction engineering, scientists can now incorporate renewable fatty acids into high-value end products, enhancing the potential of a circular bioeconomy.

title: Forcing self-metathesis reaction beyond thermodynamic equilibrium for green chemicals
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01847-2The self-cross metathesis of oleic acid esters enables synthesis of valuable, fully biosourced chemicals, but its productivity is thermodynamically limited to 50%. Here the authors overcome this limit to double the reaction yield by using a reactive distillation set-up.

title: Global patterns of reservoir sedimentation and overlooked risks in small reservoirs
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01859-yGlobal reservoirs are under threat from increasing sedimentation rates, which threaten infrastructure function and storage capacity. A focus on large reservoirs underestimates the scale of the problem and the importance of resilient small reservoirs for global water security.

title: Methane eaters cannot speed up enough
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02656-3Global warming boosts freshwater methane production. Now, a study shows that methane oxidizing bacteria cannot increase their methane consumption rates enough in response to warming-induced enhancement of methane availability, leading to higher emissions.

title: Blue carbon projects must uphold the land and sea rights of coastal peoples
abstract: Nature Climate Change, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41558-026-02643-8Blue carbon brings new interest in the land and sea rights of coastal communities, small-scale fishers and Indigenous peoples. While promises of climate mitigation, conservation and economic benefits are compelling, blue carbon projects must uphold local agency and rights as a legal and ethical duty, not just as conditional for project approval.

title: Ideological divides soften near home
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00444-wCity politics often looks sharply ideological when residents are asked about policy in the abstract. A new Canadian study shows those divides soften when the very same issues are framed as questions about one’s own neighborhood.

title: Geographic proximity dampens ideological policy disagreement in urban politics
abstract: Nature Cities, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44284-026-00450-yIdeology clearly affects politics and policy, but how about in one’s own backyard? Considering 40 municipal policy issues in Canada, this study finds that all politics are not local and that ideological disagreements dampen at neighborhood scales.

title: Energy-efficient electrified ethylene synthesis in CO-starved environment
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01849-0Electrochemical carbon monoxide (CO) reduction offers a viable approach to sustainable ethylene production, but remains challenging under low CO availability. This study shows how to achieve efficient and selective ethylene synthesis in a CO-starved environment.

title: A circular hydrothermal refinery for sustainable aviation fuel from food waste
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01848-1Decarbonizing aviation is critical for global sustainability. The authors show a circular hydrothermal refinery transforming food waste into a 100% drop-in sustainable aviation fuel rich in cycloalkanes.

title: A circular hydrothermal refinery for sustainable aviation fuel from food waste
abstract: Decarbonizing aviation is critical for global sustainability. The authors show a circular hydrothermal refinery transforming food waste into a 100% drop-in sustainable aviation fuel rich in cycloalkanes.

title: Bird migration and wind-energy production across Western Europe
abstract: Nature Sustainability, Published online: 02 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41893-026-01853-4Renewable wind energy can aid decarbonization efforts; however, the impacts of wind turbines on migrating birds remain unclear. The authors study the potential risks of wind energy installations on migrating bird populations across Western Europe and assess potential trade-offs between wind energy and conservation.

title: Bird migration and wind-energy production across Western Europe
abstract: Renewable wind energy can aid decarbonization efforts; however, the impacts of wind turbines on migrating birds remain unclear. The authors study the potential risks of wind energy installations on migrating bird populations across Western Europe and assess potential trade-offs between wind energy and conservation.
